name: npm
context: Use npm to install, manage, and publish JavaScript packages via the npm registry and configure Node.js project dependencies
info: This skill covers the npm CLI and ecosystem: initializing projects with package.json, installing and resolving dependencies from the npm registry, managing lockfiles for deterministic builds, running scripts for build and test workflows, configuring workspaces for monorepo setups, and publishing packages. npm ships with Node.js and is the default package manager for the JavaScript ecosystem. The registry at npmjs.com hosts over two million packages. Sub-skills provide focused coverage: [[node/npm/install]] for dependency installation and lockfile management, [[node/npm/publish]] for releasing packages to the registry, and [[node/npm/scripts]] for running and composing npm scripts. See [[node]] for the Node.js runtime and [[node/esm]] for ES module configuration.
body:
# npm — Node Package Manager

npm is the default package manager shipped with Node.js. It provides a CLI for installing dependencies, running scripts, and publishing packages to the npm registry at https://www.npmjs.com.

## Sub-Skills

This skill is divided into focused sub-skills for specific operations:

- **[[node/npm/install]]** — Install, update, and remove packages; manage lockfiles and dependency resolution
- **[[node/npm/publish]]** — Publish packages to the registry with versioning, access control, and provenance
- **[[node/npm/scripts]]** — Run scripts, lifecycle hooks, npx execution, and cross-platform composition

## Initializing a Project

```bash
# Create a new project with defaults
mkdir my-app && cd my-app
npm init -y

# Interactive initialization
npm init

# Scoped package
npm init --scope=@myorg

# Use an initializer package
npm init @vitejs/app my-app
npm init react-app my-app
```

## Installing Dependencies

```bash
# Install all from package.json
npm install

# Add production dependency
npm install express

# Add dev dependency
npm install -D typescript @types/express

# Add exact version (no ^ prefix)
npm install -E lodash@4.17.21

# Install globally
npm install -g tsx
```

## Running Scripts

```bash
# Run a named script
npm run build
npm run lint

# Shortcuts
npm test        # runs "test" script
npm start       # runs "start" script

# Pass arguments to a script
npm run test -- --watch

# Run across all workspaces
npm run build --workspaces
```

## package.json Anatomy

```json
{
  "name": "@scope/my-package",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs",
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format cjs,esm --dts",
    "test": "vitest",
    "lint": "eslint src/",
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run build && npm test"
  },
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {},
  "peerDependencies": {},
  "engines": { "node": ">=20" },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "private": false
}
```

### Key Fields

| Field | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `name` | Package name; scoped names use `@scope/name` |
| `version` | Semver version string |
| `type` | `"module"` for ESM, omit or `"commonjs"` for CJS |
| `main` | Entry point for CommonJS `require()` |
| `exports` | Modern conditional entry point map (preferred over main) |
| `scripts` | Named commands run via `npm run <name>` |
| `dependencies` | Production runtime dependencies |
| `devDependencies` | Build and test tools |
| `peerDependencies` | Dependencies the consumer must provide |
| `engines` | Required Node.js and npm versions |
| `files` | Whitelist of files included when publishing |
| `private` | Set `true` to prevent accidental publishing |

## Semver Ranges

| Range | Meaning |
|-------|--------|
| `^1.2.3` | Compatible with 1.x.x (>=1.2.3 <2.0.0) |
| `~1.2.3` | Patch-level only (>=1.2.3 <1.3.0) |
| `1.2.3` | Exact version |
| `*` | Any version |
| `>=1.0.0 <2.0.0` | Explicit range |
| `1.x` | Wildcard for minor and patch |

## npm Workspaces (Monorepo)

Workspaces let you manage multiple packages in a single repository.

```json
{
  "name": "my-monorepo",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"]
}
```

```bash
# Install all workspace dependencies (hoisted to root)
npm install

# Run script in one workspace
npm run build -w packages/core

# Run script in all workspaces
npm run build --workspaces

# Add a dependency to a specific workspace
npm install lodash -w packages/utils

# List workspaces
npm ls --workspaces --depth=0
```

Workspaces share a single root `node_modules` via symlinks. Cross-workspace dependencies resolve automatically when versions match.

## .npmrc Configuration

`.npmrc` files configure npm behavior at the project, user, or global level.

```ini
# .npmrc — project-level configuration
registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/
@myorg:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}

# Save exact versions by default
save-exact=true

# Set the default Node.js engine strictness
engine-strict=true

# Use a specific shell for npm scripts
script-shell=/bin/bash
```

**Precedence** (highest to lowest):
1. Command-line flags
2. Environment variables (`npm_config_<key>`)
3. Project `.npmrc` (in project root)
4. User `~/.npmrc`
5. Global `/etc/npmrc`

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `NPM_TOKEN` | Authentication token for the npm registry |
| `NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY` | Override the default registry URL |
| `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX` | Set the global install prefix |
| `NODE_ENV` | When `production`, `npm install` skips devDependencies |
| `npm_config_<key>` | Override any npm config value |
| `npm_package_name` | Available in scripts — current package name |
| `npm_package_version` | Available in scripts — current package version |
| `npm_lifecycle_event` | Available in scripts — name of the running script |

## Common Commands Reference

```bash
npm init -y                    # Initialize with defaults
npm install                    # Install all from package.json
npm install <pkg>              # Add to dependencies
npm install -D <pkg>           # Add to devDependencies
npm install -g <pkg>           # Install globally
npm update                     # Update within semver ranges
npm outdated                   # Check for outdated packages
npm audit                      # Security vulnerability check
npm audit fix                  # Auto-fix vulnerabilities
npm ls --depth=0               # List top-level packages
npm info <pkg>                 # Package metadata from registry
npm search <query>             # Search the registry
npm cache clean --force        # Clear the npm cache
npm config list                # Show active configuration
npm config set <key> <value>   # Set a config value
```

sections.title: package.json configuration
sections.body: The `package.json` file is the manifest for any Node.js project. Key fields: `name` (unique on registry; scoped names use `@scope/name`), `version` (semver string), `type` (set to `"module"` for ESM), `main` (CJS entry point), `exports` (modern conditional entry map — preferred over main/module), `scripts` (named commands via `npm run`), `dependencies` and `devDependencies` (runtime vs build-time packages), `peerDependencies` (consumer-provided deps), `engines` (required Node.js version), `files` (whitelist for publishing), and `private` (set `true` to prevent accidental publish). Use `npm init -y` to scaffold a default package.json or `npm init` for the interactive wizard.

sections.title: Lockfiles and deterministic installs
sections.body: The `package-lock.json` file pins every resolved dependency version and integrity hash, ensuring deterministic installs across machines. Always commit this file to version control. Use `npm ci` in CI pipelines — it reads the lockfile strictly and fails if it is out of sync with `package.json`. Never manually edit the lockfile. If merge conflicts occur, delete `package-lock.json` and `node_modules`, then run `npm install` to regenerate. The lockfile enables reproducible builds and prevents supply-chain drift. See [[node/npm/install]] for `npm ci` vs `npm install` details.

sections.title: npm workspaces for monorepos
sections.body: Workspaces enable managing multiple packages in a single repository. Define them in the root `package.json` with `"workspaces": ["packages/*", "apps/*"]`. Run `npm install` at the root to hoist shared dependencies and create symlinks for cross-workspace resolution. Target a specific workspace with `-w packages/core` or all workspaces with `--workspaces`. Workspaces reduce disk usage through hoisting and simplify cross-package development. Set `"private": true` on the root package to prevent accidental publishing of the monorepo root.

sections.title: .npmrc configuration
sections.body: The `.npmrc` file controls npm behavior at the project, user, or global level. Common settings: `registry` (default package source), scoped registries (`@myorg:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/`), auth tokens (`//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}`), `save-exact=true` (pin exact versions), and `engine-strict=true` (enforce engines field). Precedence order: command-line flags > env vars (`npm_config_*`) > project `.npmrc` > user `~/.npmrc` > global `/etc/npmrc`. Never commit tokens directly — use environment variable interpolation.

sections.title: Semantic versioning and ranges
sections.body: npm uses semantic versioning (semver) for dependency resolution. Format: `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`. MAJOR for breaking changes, MINOR for backwards-compatible features, PATCH for backwards-compatible fixes. Range operators: `^1.2.3` (caret — allows minor and patch), `~1.2.3` (tilde — allows patch only), `>=1.0.0` (comparison), `1.x` (wildcard), and `1.0.0 - 2.0.0` (hyphen range). Pre-release tags like `1.0.0-beta.1` sort before the release version. The lockfile pins exact resolved versions regardless of range specifiers.

links.context: ES module configuration for type: module

related.note: Node.js must be installed — npm ships bundled with the Node.js runtime
ES module configuration affects how packages are loaded and the type field in package.json

scripts.name: Initialize npm Project
scripts.description: Create a new npm project directory with a configured package.json and sensible defaults

projectName.description: Name for the new project directory and package

private.description: Whether to mark the package as private

type.description: Module system — module for ESM, commonjs for CJS

packageJsonPath.description: Absolute path to the created package.json

name.description: The resolved package name written to package.json
